results.title: Alternative software to PassMark Performance Test - Software & Applications - Spiceworks Community
results.content:
Allows you to compare against similar builds as well as GPU to GPU / CPU to CPU of like models online.

UserBenchmark is another alternative you can use as well: UserBenchmark: PC Speed Test Tool - Compare Your PC

The most sophisticated tool I have ever seen is AIDA64 Engineer AIDA64 Engineer | AIDA64 . The number of various benchmarks is overwhelming. The final report counts over 50 pages and can be exported to various formats. There is an online DB as well.

It is a commercial tool, but for one-time purposes, there is a trial version, and a beta version can be used for free if I remember it correctly.

Windows Repair Toolbox has some benchmark tools free to use for stress testing (CPU, RAM, HDD, GPU)

### Windows Repair Toolbox [...] # Alternative software to PassMark Performance Test

Hello,

We need a software like PassMark Performance Test for benchmarking, which includes a final score and maybe the possibility to compare it online.  
The most important thing is the result exportation in html or pdf and others.

Do you have any advice for us?

Thank you in advance.

I may feel that you may want to share more details like what are you “benchmarking” ?

Most “benchmarking” are done on controlled sets of machines with controlled configs.

We are banchmarking standard computer components (CPU, RAM, HDD, 2D, 3D).

NovaBench:

### Novabench - Free Computer Benchmark Software

Benchmarking Alternative Investments

cfa-level-iii

# Benchmarking Alternative Investments

11 Nov 2023

Alter alternative investments are notoriously difficult to benchmark. The selection of an appropriate benchmark is made difficult by:

 Lack of high-quality/investible market indexes.
 Frequent use of leverage.
 Limited liquidity.
 Lack of readily available market values.
 Use of IRR rather than time-weighted rates of return. [...] 1. The benchmarks are based on a subset of the real estate opportunity set only.
2. Index performance is likely to be highly by the largest fund data contributors.
3. Benchmark returns are based on self-reported performance.
4. Benchmarks weighted by fund or asset value may place a disproportionate emphasis on the most expensive cities and asset types.
5. Valuations of the underlying properties are typically based on appraisals because there are few transactions to measure.
6. Varying degrees of leverage are used.
7. Real estate indexes do not reflect the high transaction costs, limited transparency, and lack of liquidity that drive performance for actual real estate investments. [...] The following sections will discuss these issues as they pertain to specific types of alternative investments.

## Hedge Funds

Hedge funds are not an asset class themselves, but rather an amalgamation of many different asset classes. Hedge fund managers employ a diverse set of strategies in managing the assets under their control, and seek to earn returns in a variety of different ways. This lack of similarity is the first hurdle to creating a meaningful benchmark.

Leverage is another common issue complicating the selection of a benchmark. Hedge funds may lever their capital base many times over, rendering them much less comparable to another fund which employs little or no leverage.

results.title: Kafka benchmark—Use cases, examples, and alternatives
results.content:
### Demonstrating performance

You can use a benchmark to determine how well a particular tool performs. Because of its repeatable nature, claims made using a benchmark are verifiable independently of the party making the claims and hence are more trustworthy. Creating a benchmark can also help the software makers better understand their own software and its performance, uncovering previously overlooked behavioral traits and helping to fix them.

### Quality-testing software updates [...] ## What is benchmarking?

Benchmarking software is the process of creating a standard, repeatable set of tests that you can use to measure the performance of the software. This test can then be used to compare other versions of the software or other software applications that perform the same function. [...] Increasing write throughput causes latencies to increase. This is expected since the Kafka cluster is under more load figuring out where the messages need to be routed and making them available from the right node and partition. Using more powerful machines and larger clusters helps improve performance. This is how analyzing benchmark test results can help you understand the performance of a tool and set expectations in an actual production environment.

[CTA\_MODULE]

## Redpanda - a simple yet powerful alternative

results.title: The Best Benchmarking Software for Accurate Hardware Performance Testing | GPUScore
results.content:
GPUscore by Basemark

### GPUScore by Basemark

Website:  

GPUScore provides a comprehensive suite of advanced gaming benchmarks designed for a wide range of devices, from mobile platforms to high-performance desktops. Since 2015, Basemark’s graphics benchmarking division, now known as GPUScore, has been at the forefront of developing accurate and reliable GPU benchmarks.  

Pro’s 

 Offers a broad range of benchmarks, including cross-platform, mobile & desktop-specific options, and ray tracing benchmarks.
 All benchmarks are free to download and use for noncommercial purposes
 Great alternative benchmark

Con’s 

 Not as widely recognized as some other benchmark providers

3DMark

### 3DMark (PCMark)

Website: [...] Unigine

### Unigine Benchmarks

Website:  

Established in 2005, Unigine Benchmark provides solutions for various 3D technologies, including visualization, simulations, and virtual reality systems. GPU benchmarks are one of their primary offerings, with Superposition being among the most popular. 

Pro’s 

 The basic edition of their benchmarks are free to use
 A paid premium edition is available for enhanced features

Con’s 

 The selection of benchmarks is somewhat limited compared to other benchmark providers
 The latest benchmark version was released in 2017, which means it may not reflect the latest GPU technologies

Geekbench

### GeekBench (Primate Labs)

Website: [...] You will often find different versions of the same benchmark tailored for various device categories. That is because different devices may have different requirements and characteristics, such as smartphone versus desktop GPU. A less common approach for benchmark providers to offer cross-platform benchmarks. Cross-platform benchmarks let you test and compare performance across different device types and environments, e.g. a PC versus an Android phone. Examples of such benchmarks include Breaking Limit or Sacred Path. 

People run benchmarks for a variety of reasons, from understanding hardware performance to optimizing software. If you want to learn more about why benchmarking matters, check out our article: The Purpose of GPU Benchmarking.

results.title: Novabench - Free PC Benchmark and Stress Test Software
results.content:
giving you immediate context for how your system performs relative to similar hardware. [...] Novabench is a free benchmarking, stress testing, and monitoring application for Windows, macOS, and Linux. It tests the core components of your computer - CPU, GPU, memory, storage, and NPU - using workloads designed for modern hardware. Each test produces a score that represents real-world capability, not just theoretical throughput. Beyond benchmarking, Novabench includes network speed testing with download, upload, latency, and jitter measurements, real-time sensor monitoring for temperatures, power draw, and battery health, and configurable stress tests to push your system under sustained load. Results are presented with clear explanations and can be compared against millions of validated results in the Novabench database, giving you immediate context for how your system performs [...] ### 

### How does Novabench compare to other benchmark tools?

Novabench is designed to give you a complete picture of your system in one tool. Where most benchmark utilities focus on a single component or a single type of test, Novabench covers CPU, GPU, memory, storage, NPU, network speed, stress testing, and sensor monitoring in a single application. Tests run in minutes rather than hours, and results include plain-language explanations alongside the numbers. The comparison database with millions of results means you get immediate context - not just a score, but an understanding of where your system stands. Novabench is also cross-platform, so you can compare results across Windows, macOS, and Linux using the same test suite and scoring methodology.

###
